page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the n2d server family. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core of an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and overall PassMark CPU scores fall into the weak category. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with cached small-block read-only bandwidth and PassMark memory scores ranking in the bottom 10%. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, while multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and database operations show poor performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2d-standard-2 offers an entry-level dedicated hardware profile suitable for lightweight workloads that do not require high memory bandwidth or parallel processing power. Given the poor multi-core scaling, low database operation throughput, and weak static web serving performance, this instance is best suited for basic general-purpose tasks, single-threaded utility scripts, lightweight text processing, or development environments rather than high-throughput web serving or intensive database applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs on 1 physical core, 8.0 GB of RAM, and utilizes the x86_64 architecture. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU capabilities, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests, alongside average single-threaded compression performance. However, it exhibits weak multi-core performance and poor memory bandwidth, with database operations and static web serving throughput falling into the bottom 10%. Qualitatively, this instance provides a cost-efficient entry point for dedicated compute resources. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, single-threaded processing tasks, and basic development environments that do not demand high memory throughput or parallel processing power.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-2 is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, offering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-2 is a 2-vCPU, 8 GB RAM general-purpose instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU and dedicated resources.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU virtual machine with strong single-core performance for lightweight general-purpose workloads and single-threaded tasks.

Strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
